about_p1: Karumanta is the name I received at the Fire ceremony in the Colombian jungles of Putumayo.
about_p2: «Karumanta» means «from afar». And it's true — I came to these lands from afar: from the Ural Mountains, where dense forests stand, where mountain rivers flow with cold water, where the legends of my people still breathe.
about_p3: Since childhood I dreamed of traveling to distant lands and writing books. I grew up in a small village where the world seemed narrow, but as soon as I opened a book — the borders disappeared. Stories were my guides, friends, roads to other worlds. Turning the page, I set off on a journey without tickets, without borders.
about_p4: And one day I understood: these are the very books I want to create myself — those that take you far away, but bring you back a little more alive than before.
about_p5: Once, as a child, I made myself a vow: one day I would set off on a journey through the jungle with just one backpack on my shoulders. But the whirlwind of life swept me up, and that vow was lost in the everyday.
about_p6: I lived, studied in New York, walked the roads of the big world — and drifted further from the girl who dreamed of wild trails and distant horizons.
about_p7: And yet dreams know how to wait. They don't disappear — they become the road that one day finds you anyway.
about_instagram_bio: moments of life, Latin America — Russia.
